title: Lysiloma polyphyllum
taxonomy: Lysiloma polyphyllum is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its compound leaves and ability to fix nitrogen through symbiotic relationships with rhizobia bacteria. The genus Lysiloma is further divided into several species, with Lysiloma polyphyllum being one of the lesser-known species. The classification of Lysiloma polyphyllum within the Fabaceae family is consistent with its morphological and anatomical features. Additionally, this classification is supported by phylogenetic studies that have investigated the evolutionary relationships among various legume species.

In some regions, Lysiloma polyphyllum is used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and indigestion. intro: Lysiloma polyphyllum, also known as the Guanacaste tree, is a tropical tree species native to Central and South America. The Guanacaste tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and expectorant properties, which are beneficial for relieving congestion and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents of Lysiloma polyphyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the Guanacaste tree is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being used in the form of a cough syrup or expectorant to alleviate respiratory issues.
